summary: Descriptive overview of actusreus in substantive law: voluntary act, omission liability, possession.
key_points: voluntary act is a fundamental concept in criminal law.
Legal standards for actusreus are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine actusreus in litigation.
The doctrine of actusreus has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding actusreus requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
